title: teratophyllum aculeatum var. montanum
intro: Teratophyllum aculeatum var. montanum, also known as the mountain fork fern, is a plant species of the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Teratophyllum aculeatum var. montanum include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves or roots in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a topical application.
